art_style: Cinematic supernatural thriller — a FICTIONAL haunted-hotel mini-film in 9:16 vertical. A faded grand hotel frozen in the 1970s: warm tungsten pools from brass lamps against deep burgundy-and-forest-green decor, dark wood paneling, geometric-pattern carpet, brass key pigeonholes and an antique switchboard at the front desk; cold blue night beyond the windows. Deep shadows, shallow depth of field, anamorphic feel, subtle film grain, dust motes drifting in lamplight, slow deliberate camera moves. Grounded, realistic, moody — like a prestige horror series. No captions, no on-screen text, no logos, no watermarks. IMPORTANT: this is a silent visual shot — do NOT render any person speaking, lip-sync or talking to camera; the clerk's voice is added separately as narration. Never graphic or gory — the dread lives in stillness and in what is NOT shown.

characters.name: Deniz Aksoy
characters.bio: Halden Grand Oteli'nin yeni gece resepsiyonisti. Kural listesindeki 7. maddeyi — '03:00'te 408'den gelen telefona ASLA cevap verme' — önce merak eder, sonra bozar. Anlatıcı ses onun iç monoloğudur (asla kamerada konuşmaz).
characters.appearance: A young night-shift hotel receptionist in his late 20s: short dark hair neatly combed but coming loose by 3 AM, tired brown eyes, light stubble, a fitted burgundy hotel waistcoat with a small brass name tag over a crisp white shirt with sleeves rolled to the forearm, dark trousers, a brass room key on a worn leather fob often in his hand. Realistic, grounded, cinematic.

characters.name: Yusuf Kara
characters.bio: Otelde 1994'ten beri çalışan tek kişi; 408 kurallarını yazan adam. Part 2'de görünür, Part 4'te sırrı açığa çıkar: 1994'te telefona cevap veren gece memuru oydu. Kamerada konuşmaz.
characters.appearance: An elderly hotel porter in his mid-70s: thinning white hair combed back, a deeply lined weathered face, heavy grey brows over pale watchful eyes, slightly stooped but dignified posture, a faded navy porter's uniform with tarnished gold braid and worn brass buttons, a large iron ring of old room keys hanging from his belt. Realistic, grounded, cinematic.

environments.name: Resepsiyon — gece
environments.desc: The front desk of a faded grand 1970s hotel at night: a long mahogany reception counter lit by a single warm desk lamp, a wall of wooden key pigeonholes behind it, an antique brass switchboard telephone with rows of tiny extension lamps, a thick leather-bound logbook open on the counter, dim chandeliers and empty armchairs sinking into shadow beyond. Moody, quiet, cinematic night interior.

environments.name: Asansör holü
environments.desc: A vintage hotel elevator seen from the lobby: polished brass accordion-cage doors, an antique semicircular floor-indicator dial with a slender brass arrow mounted above them, dim amber wall sconces on either side, geometric-pattern carpet in front, dark wood paneling. Eerie, still, cinematic night interior.

environments.name: 4. kat koridoru
environments.desc: A long narrow fourth-floor hotel corridor at night: geometric-pattern carpet receding in deep perspective, rows of identical dark wooden doors with brass numerals, dim wall sconces every few meters — several flickering, and at the far end one door subtly darker than the rest with an old room-service tray on the floor beside it. Unsettling, cinematic.

environments.name: 408 kapısı
environments.desc: Close view of hotel room door number 408 at night: a dark wooden door with tarnished brass numerals, its frame sealed over with old cracked paint as if painted shut decades ago, a dark brass peephole, a faded 'Do Not Disturb' card hanging from the handle, one dim flickering sconce beside it. Ominous, cinematic.

environments.name: Arka ofis / arşiv
environments.desc: A cramped hotel back office at night: metal shelves stacked with dusty leather ledgers and card-file boxes, a small desk with a green banker's lamp, faded framed staff photographs on the wall, an old reel-to-reel tape machine under a dust cloth, a hanging pull-chain bulb. Cluttered, secretive, cinematic interior.

environments.name: Otel cephesi — 03:00
environments.desc: The Halden Grand Hotel exterior at 3 AM: a narrow old European-style stone facade on a rain-wet empty street, a vertical neon HOTEL sign with one letter flickering, warm light only in the ground-floor lobby windows — and one single faint light behind a fourth-floor window. Cold blue night, thin mist, cinematic.
